rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.note: Source domain Wikipedia page; not LLM prior art.
CAMON decentralized LLM multi-agent navigation with dynamic leadership organization - direct analog to cluster-navigator relay primitive.
CONSENSAGENT multi-agent LLM consensus via dynamic prompt refinement - consensus primitive overlaps with cross-agent drift correction.
Multi-Agent Consensus Seeking via LLMs - same consensus aggregation primitive as PIAILUG-FLEET's cross-agent consensus correction.
Adaptive Behavioral Anchoring (ABA) - few-shot exemplar anchoring + drift-aware routing is direct functional twin of fixed-axis drift correction; STRONGEST overlap.
LLM Output Drift cross-provider validation mitigation - same drift detection/correction effect cluster.
Provable Coordination via Message Sequence Charts - multi-agent coordination protocol artefact overlap.
Coordination Architectural Layer with prediction-market style information control - multi-agent coordination primitive overlap.

fail_reason: 7 of 8 results (only excluding the source-domain Wikipedia page) cross the sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7 thresholds. Most damaging: arxiv 2601.04170 Agent Drift (ABA) at sem=0.85, func=0.88 - Adaptive Behavioral Anchoring with drift-aware routing is a direct functional twin of PIAILUG-FLEET's frozen-axis projection + drift correction mechanism. Additionally CAMON (cluster-navigator analog), CONSENSAGENT and Multi-Agent Consensus Seeking (consensus primitive), LLM Output Drift (drift mitigation), and two coordination-layer papers all match on the multi-agent consensus + drift correction effect cluster. The 'frozen reference axis' claim does not differentiate enough - ABA's exemplar anchoring serves the same role. Confirms 07_hit_miss.json: 7 hits, FAIL.
